hero.label: Made for Tractian
hero.headline: I want to bring Physical AI to the shop floor.
hero.subheadline: Tractian is solving a problem that most software companies ignore entirely: the machines that actually make things. Igor building this after watching his father struggle as a maintenance technician is the kind of founder conviction that makes a product real. I want to be the person opening those doors for manufacturers who still think downtime is just the cost of doing business.

whyCompany.title: Tractian owns hardware and software, which means the pitch is airtight
whyCompany.body: Most IIoT vendors sell sensors or software. Tractian builds both, which means no finger-pointing when something breaks and a much stronger value story to tell a maintenance manager. That kind of end-to-end ownership makes cold outreach cleaner because the product actually delivers what you promise.

whyCompany.title: 158% NRR means every new logo compounds, not churns
whyCompany.body: Tractian's net revenue retention rivals Snowflake and Twilio, which tells me customers are expanding after they buy. For an SDR, that means the accounts I open are not one-and-done wins but the start of something that grows. Joining now, while the sales team is still scaling, is exactly the moment where early reps build careers.

whyCompany.title: My BDR experience at Copygen.ai maps directly to this motion
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai I ran cold outreach from scratch, built LinkedIn sequences, managed HubSpot pipelines, and helped land 15 to 20 paying clients in a market that had no idea we existed yet. That is the same muscle Tractian needs: finding manufacturing decision-makers who have never heard of predictive maintenance software and turning skepticism into a booked demo.

quote: Igor's father was the maintenance tech. I want to be the person who makes sure the next generation of maintenance techs has Tractian in their corner. That story is worth making 100 calls a day for.
